title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Chicken Recipe: Authentic Korean Fried Chicken
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Yangnyeom Chicken with our easy recipe! Get crispy, sweet & spicy Korean fried chicken at home. Perfect for dinner or parties.
longTailKeywords: how to make Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken
easy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: sweet and spicy Korean chicken wings
seo.h1: Authentic Yangnyeom Chicken: Korean Fried Chicken Recipe
h2Headings: What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?
Ingredients You Need for Yangnyeom Chicken
How to Make Crispy Korean Fried Chicken
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Plate of crispy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken coated in sweet and spicy sauce, garnished with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the perfect balance of sweet and spicy with our homemade Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken!

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring impossibly crispy fried chicken coated in a luscious, sticky, sweet, and spicy sauce. This iconic Korean comfort food offers a delightful textural contrast and an explosion of flavors that will transport your taste buds straight to Seoul. Perfect for sharing with friends and family, it's a truly addictive treat.

ingredients.notes: cut into bite-sized pieces if using thighs

ingredients.item: Very cold water or sparkling water

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken and Marinade
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. In a large bowl, combine the chicken with 1 tablespoon soy sauce, grated ginger, minced garlic, and black pepper. Mix well and let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a separate large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, potato starch, baking powder, and salt. In another small bowl, whisk the egg with the very cold water. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix gently until just combined. A few lumps are fine; do not overmix, as this will result in tough chicken. The batter should be thick enough to coat the chicken.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large deep pot or Dutch oven to 170°C (340°F). Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated, and carefully lower it into the hot oil. Fry the chicken in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pot, for 6-8 minutes until lightly golden and cooked through. Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Repeat with remaining chicken.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is draining, prepare the sauce. In a medium saucepan, combine gochujang, ketchup, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, sugar or corn syrup, minced garlic, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and water. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens slightly and becomes glossy, about 3-5 minutes. Set aside.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Extra Crispy Chicken
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 180°C (355°F). Carefully return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 2-3 minutes until the chicken is deeply golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying technique is key to achieving the signature Korean fried chicken crunch. Remove the chicken and place it back on the wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat and Serve
instructions.text: Once all the chicken is double-fried, transfer it to a large mixing bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently with tongs until every piece is evenly coated. Garnish immediately with toasted sesame seeds and crushed peanuts, if using. Serve hot and enjoy the perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and crispy!

tips: For the crispiest chicken, ensure your batter is made with very cold water or sparkling water. The cold temperature creates a greater contrast with the hot oil, leading to a lighter, crispier crust. Also, do not overcrowd the fryer.

Adjust the spice level of your Yangnyeom sauce by increasing or decreasing the amount of gochujang. For a sweeter sauce, you can add a touch more corn syrup or sugar. Using Korean corn syrup (mulyeot) will give a more authentic glossy finish to the sauce.

Yangnyeom chicken is best enjoyed immediately after coating to maintain its crispiness. If you have leftovers, store the chicken and sauce separately and combine just before reheating in an air fryer or oven for best results.
article: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved Korean Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
